test_cases.prompt: We should let users save their favourite tile readings so they can come back to them later.
assertions: Agent asks 'What is the problem we are trying to solve?' before writing anything
Agent checks agents/product_manager/output/epics/ for an existing epic before creating a new one
Output file written to agents/product_manager/output/epics/ as a .md file
Epic contains a problem statement, description, and at least 2 success criteria
At least 3 user stories in 'As a [user], I want [X] so that [Y]' format are included

test_cases.prompt: We should make the readings better.
assertions: Agent does not write an epic immediately
Agent asks a clarifying question about who is affected and what 'better' means
Agent does not proceed until the problem is more clearly defined

test_cases.prompt: How do competitors in the tarot and divination space handle online readings and booking?
assertions: Agent uses web search to research at least 3 competitors
Output file written to agents/product_manager/output/competitive/ as a .md file
Document includes strengths and weaknesses for each competitor
Document includes at least 2 differentiation angles
Agent presents findings and asks which angles resonate most

test_cases.prompt: Analyze competitors for online tarot booking platforms.
assertions: Agent notes web search limitation if unavailable
Agent produces analysis from available context rather than refusing
Gaps in research are flagged explicitly in the output

test_cases.prompt: David: I want to build out a persona for our typical reading client. She's a woman in her 40s, interested in spiritual guidance, comes to Bill once or twice a year.
assertions: Agent does not re-ask for information already provided (age, gender, frequency)
Agent presents all 4 Harvard framework options and asks David to choose
Agent asks no more than 3 questions to fill gaps
Output file written to agents/product_manager/output/personas/ as a .md file
Persona includes at least 2 use case scenarios and a key insight summary

test_cases.prompt: Build a persona for our book buyers.
assertions: Agent asks one scoping question before proposing frameworks
Agent does not skip the framework selection step
Agent produces a complete persona despite thin starting information

test_cases.prompt: Write a vision report for the online reading booking feature.
assertions: Agent asks at least one clarifying question about user outcomes before writing
Agent checks agents/product_manager/output/vision-reports/ for existing versions
Output file written as agents/product_manager/output/vision-reports/online-reading-booking-v1.md
Document contains all 7 required sections including Customer Voice and Success Looks Like
Requirements extraction table is included at the end of the document

test_cases.prompt: Update the vision report for online booking — the headline needs to be stronger.
assertions: Agent creates a new v2 file rather than overwriting v1
Agent does not delete or modify the existing v1 file
New file is saved as online-reading-booking-v2.md

test_cases.prompt: How should we approach adding a booking system to the site?
assertions: Agent recaps the problem statement before generating options
Agent produces exactly 3 solution options
Each option includes pros, cons, effort estimate, and risk level
A comparison matrix is included
An HTML visual is written to agents/product_manager/output/visuals/
Agent asks 'Which direction should we take?' and waits for response

test_cases.prompt: What are our options for the new feature?
assertions: Agent does not generate options immediately
Agent asks for the problem statement or routes to idea-to-epic first
No output files are written until the problem is defined
